rolling_30d.summary: FRE is experiencing robust growth across all major traffic channels, with sessions up 39.3% MoM and 115.1% YoY over the last 30 days. However, engagement rate declined 5.0 pp MoM and 13.2 pp YoY despite a 25.2% MoM increase in engaged sessions, indicating that traffic volume growth is outpacing quality engagement—a healthy scaling signal but one requiring monitoring. High-value conversions are performing exceptionally: mainsite_clicks (+23.2% MoM, +79.0% YoY), shop_now (+23.8% MoM, +81.9% YoY), and location-based events (store_locate +23.2% MoM, locate_me +31.9% MoM) are all trending strongly, suggesting users are progressing deeper into the funnel. Direct traffic dominates at 59.1% of sessions with a 48.3% MoM and 128.1% YoY surge, while organic search remains steady at 34.3% with more modest 9.3% MoM growth. State-level landing pages show mixed performance: /florida and /texas are explosive (YoY +422.7% and +343.8% respectively), while /texas declined 35.2% MoM despite strong YoY gains. YTD 2026 sits at 95,782 sessions (+95.5% vs 2025), with March showing particular strength at +110.4% YoY, though February's growth decelerated to +35.9% YoY.

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on location-intent momentum
quick_wins.detail: store_locate (8,965 events, +23.2% MoM) and get_directions (1,106 events, +42.0% MoM) are accelerating faster than overall traffic, signaling strong local search behavior. Doubling down on location-based content and mobile optimization for state pages could amplify these high-intent actions.

quick_wins.title: Leverage direct traffic dominance
quick_wins.detail: Direct traffic (21,420 sessions, +48.3% MoM, +128.1% YoY) is the primary driver and growing faster than organic search. This suggests strong brand recall and repeat visits; invest in retention campaigns and email nurture to sustain this channel.

quick_wins.title: Optimize homepage engagement
quick_wins.detail: The homepage (/) captures 45.5% of all sessions (16,497) with a healthy 65.9% engagement rate. Small improvements to CTA clarity and pathway to mainsite_clicks could unlock significant conversion gains.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement rate deterioration despite volume growth
potential_warnings.detail: Engagement rate dropped 5.0 pp MoM and 13.2 pp YoY while sessions surged 39.3% MoM and 115.1% YoY, indicating incoming traffic quality may be declining or user experience friction is increasing. Monitor scroll depth, time-on-page, and bounce rate by channel to diagnose.

potential_warnings.title: Texas landing page volatility
potential_warnings.detail: /texas declined 35.2% MoM despite strong 343.8% YoY growth, suggesting either seasonal dips or recent traffic shifts away from this state page. Investigate whether organic search visibility or paid campaigns targeting Texas have changed.

potential_warnings.title: Unassigned traffic explosion requires investigation
potential_warnings.detail: Unassigned traffic spiked 815.1% MoM and 1096.1% YoY to 2,727 sessions (7.5% of total), potentially masking true channel attribution. This could be a GA4 tracking issue or a sign that data validation needs review.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale organic search investment
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search (12,432 sessions, 34.3% of total) is growing only 9.3% MoM versus direct's 48.3% MoM. Prioritizing SEO improvements on high-performing state pages (/california, /florida, /georgia) could capture market share and reduce reliance on direct traffic.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand paid search and paid social
biggest_opportunities.detail: Paid search (60 sessions, +62.2% MoM) and paid social (28 sessions, +211.1% MoM) are negligible but accelerating. These channels have massive headroom and could diversify traffic away from the direct-dominated mix.

biggest_opportunities.title: Diagnose and fix New York engagement gap
biggest_opportunities.detail: /new-york has only 23.8% engagement rate (lowest among state pages) despite 78.7% MoM growth in sessions. This suggests traffic quality or content relevance issues; A/B testing page layout or messaging could significantly improve conversion efficiency.

biggest_opportunities.title: Investigate February growth deceleration
biggest_opportunities.detail: February 2026 sessions (+35.9% YoY) significantly underperformed January (+155.0% YoY) and March (+110.4% YoY). Identify whether external factors (campaign pauses, seasonality, competition) drove the slowdown and ensure March momentum is sustained.

last_calendar_month.summary: FRE delivered exceptional growth in March 2026, with sessions up 53.4% MoM and 110.4% YoY, reaching 37,184 total sessions. YTD 2026 has already eclipsed 95,782 sessions — a 95.5% increase versus YTD 2025 — signaling sustained momentum after a record January (+155.0% YoY). However, engagement rate declined 5.2 pp MoM and 13.4 pp YoY to 43.7%, indicating that traffic growth is outpacing quality engagement, likely driven by higher volumes of lower-intent Direct traffic (59.3% of sessions, +64.1% MoM). High-value conversion signals remain strong: mainsite_clicks (+35.7% MoM, +78.6% YoY), shop_now (+36.5% MoM, +81.4% YoY), and store_locate (+35.1% MoM, +43.0% YoY) all grew substantially. Location-intent events (store_locate, locate_me, get_directions) collectively show healthy demand, with locate_me particularly strong (+43.8% MoM). Regional landing pages—especially /florida, /texas, and /california—are driving outsized engagement and YoY growth, suggesting effective localized traffic acquisition or seasonal demand. A critical anomaly: Unassigned traffic exploded +910.3% MoM to 2,748 sessions, requiring immediate audit to ensure proper channel attribution.

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on regional landing page momentum
quick_wins.detail: /florida, /texas, and /california are delivering strong engagement (46.5%–62.4%) with explosive YoY growth (+95.7%, +380.6%, +395.7% respectively). Consider amplifying paid search and organic optimization for these high-intent state pages.

quick_wins.title: Leverage Direct traffic volume for retargeting
quick_wins.detail: Direct now represents 59.3% of sessions (+64.1% MoM). Implement audience segmentation and personalization on homepage to convert these high-volume but lower-engagement visitors into mainsite_clicks and location actions.

quick_wins.title: Expand store locator UX investment
quick_wins.detail: store_locate (9,156 events), locate_me (7,311), and get_directions (1,116) collectively signal strong location-seeking intent. All three grew 32–52% MoM; doubling down on frictionless location lookup could convert this intent into foot traffic.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement rate compression despite session growth
potential_warnings.detail: Engagement rate fell 5.2 pp MoM and 13.4 pp YoY despite 53.4% MoM session growth, indicating new traffic is lower-quality or arriving via less-engaged channels. This suggests organic acquisition may be pulling in higher-funnel traffic that requires nurturing.

potential_warnings.title: Unassigned traffic spike requires urgent audit
potential_warnings.detail: Unassigned traffic jumped 910.3% MoM to 2,748 sessions (7.4% of total), obscuring true channel performance and undermining attribution analysis. Investigate GA4 configuration, server-side tagging, and UTM consistency immediately.

potential_warnings.title: Organic Search underperforming relative to session growth
potential_warnings.detail: Organic Search grew only 19.8% MoM while overall sessions grew 53.4%, indicating Direct is driving most gains. If Direct growth is unsustainable, organic rankings and paid efforts must be prioritized to stabilize acquisition.

biggest_opportunities.title: Optimize Organic Search for location queries
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Search is only 34.1% of traffic but commands strong YoY growth (+75.7%). Targeting state-level and local keyword clusters (e.g., 'FRE [state]') paired with /california, /texas, /florida landing pages could unlock significant volume at high intent.

biggest_opportunities.title: Resurrect Paid Search program
biggest_opportunities.detail: Paid Search represents just 59 sessions (0.2%) despite +247.1% YoY growth and strong YoY ROI signals. Even a modest $500–1,000/month budget targeting high-intent 'book now' and location keywords could yield outsized returns given mainsite_clicks and shop_now momentum.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand Paid Social beyond current footprint
biggest_opportunities.detail: Paid Social grew 250% MoM but remains negligible at 28 sessions. Given state landing pages' strong engagement and Direct audience scale, a targeted Paid Social campaign to these segments could recapture high-intent traffic at lower CAC than Direct.
